name: Can I get extra money for pain and suffering if the equipment was old and broken?
description: Workers\' comp doesn\'t pay for pain and suffering, but a third-party lawsuit against the equipment manufacturer or a negligent maintenance company can provide

mainEntity.name: Can I get extra money for pain and suffering if the equipment was old and broken?

acceptedAnswer.text: Workers\' comp doesn\'t pay for pain and suffering, but a third-party lawsuit against the equipment manufacturer or a negligent maintenance company can provide these additional damages.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Standard workers\' compensation in Utah and Idaho does not pay for "pain and suffering." It is a trade-off: you get medical bills paid without proving fault, but you give up the right to sue your employer for emotional distress. However, if a third party (like a manufacturer) is involved, a lawsuit against them *does* include pain and suffering.

This is why identifying a malfunction is so important. It opens the door to money that workers\' comp alone cannot provide.

Key factors include:
- The severity of your physical pain and duration of recovery.
- Impact on your mental health (PTSD, anxiety).
- Loss of enjoyment of hobbies or family life.
- Permanent scarring or disfigurement.

If your injury is severe, relying solely on workers\' comp may leave you financially struggling. An attorney can evaluate if a third-party claim exists to help you get the full compensation you deserve for your physical and mental suffering.
